# Test fixtures: trimmed DISA XCCDF audit-rule extracts

Each `<product>_auditd_controls.xml` is a trimmed slice of the real, official,
publicly-releasable DISA STIG XCCDF benchmark for that product, containing only
the `<Group>`/`<Rule>` elements the au-W06 selector cares about, plus two
decoy Groups per file (an `auditd.conf` key=value check and a service/package
check) so the selector-exclusion tests have real, non-synthetic negative
fixtures. `check-content` and `fixtext` text is copied VERBATIM from the source
benchmark (byte-for-byte after entity decoding); `description`/`reference`/
`ident` elements are stripped (the parser never reads them). Non-ASCII
typographic characters in the source prose (curly quotes, en/em dashes,
ellipsis, nbsp) are transliterated to ASCII equivalents so the fixture files
satisfy the repo's ASCII-only convention; this is prose-only and never touches
a `-a`/`-A`/`-w` rule line, none of which contain non-ASCII characters in any
of the three source benchmarks.

DEV-ONLY: these fixtures exist to make `tools/auditd-stig-update`'s tests and
the `auditd-stig-check` CI gate run OFFLINE, without depending on the DISA CDN
being reachable during a PR. They are not shipped in the `rulesteward` release
binary or its distribution artifacts.

## Provenance

| Product | Source benchmark | Selected requirements | Extracted lines | Decoys |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| rhel8  | RHEL 8 STIG V2R4 (02 Jul 2025), `U_RHEL_8_V2R4_STIG.zip`  | 45 | 61 | 2 |
| rhel9  | RHEL 9 STIG V2R7 (05 Jan 2026), `U_RHEL_9_V2R7_STIG.zip`  | 51 | 67 | 2 |
| rhel10 | RHEL 10 STIG V1R1 (26 Feb 2026), `U_RHEL_10_V1R1_STIG.zip` | 50 | 75 | 2 |

These are the SAME three DISA zip filenames `tools/sshd-stig-update/stig-refs.toml`
already pins (the auditd and sshd baselines are drawn from the same three
benchmark documents); see `../../stig-refs.toml` in this tool for the pinned
CDN URL.

## Selector (which Groups are included)

A `<Group>`/`<Rule>` pair is included as a "selected" (audit-rule requirement)
fixture entry IFF its `check-content` has at least one line (after trimming
whitespace) matching `^(-A|-a|-w)\s+\S`, i.e. a literal `auditctl`-syntax rule
line -- the same selector grounded in
`.private-docs/orchestration/../grounding/auditd-stig/` session 7c-v0_6-wave3
P2 grounding doc, Part B.2/B.3 (RULE_LINE_RE). Extraction is from
`check-content`, NOT `fixtext` (a deliberate, grounded deviation from the
`sshd-stig-update` precedent, which sources `fixtext`; see the P2 grounding
doc Part B.4 for the mechanical evidence: `fixtext` and `check-content`
literally disagree -- missing `-S all`, a different `auid` sentinel spelling,
`-k` vs `-F key=` -- for a majority of the rhel9/rhel10 requirements).

The two decoys per file are real DISA Groups that were NOT selected: one whose
`check-content` targets `/etc/audit/auditd.conf` (a `key = value` config check,
not a rule line) and one that checks the `auditd` service/package
(`systemctl is-enabled auditd`, `rpm -q audit`). Their presence proves the
selector's exclusion logic actually filters something, rather than passing
every Group through.

## Regenerating

These files were generated by a one-off script against the cached benchmarks
at `/home/runner/rulesteward-docs/grounding/auditd-stig/stig_research/`
(gitignored docs tree; not part of this repo). If the pinned DISA revision
bumps, regenerate by re-running the same selector over the new benchmark and
reviewing the diff before committing -- do not hand-edit these files.
